subtopic: Softshell Turtles and New Fish Species
title: Operation Olivia
focus: Indian Coast Guard initiative for turtle conservation in Odisha.
rationale: Standalone conservation operation conducted by the Indian Coast Guard, highly relevant for prelims.
sectionExcerpt: Initiated in 1980s by Indian Coast Guard, conducted every year from November to May. Focuses on Gahirmatha Beach, Rushikulya River Mouth, and Devi River Mouth.
evidenceBullets: Initiated in 1980s by Indian Coast Guard
Conducted annually from November to May
Focuses on Gahirmatha Beach, Rushikulya River Mouth, and Devi River Mouth

subtopic: Softshell Turtles and New Fish Species
title: Asian Giant Softshell Turtle
focus: Habitat, conservation status, and characteristics of the Asian Giant Softshell Turtle.
rationale: Critically endangered species in news due to an award-winning documentary; important for environment and biodiversity questions.
sectionExcerpt: Found in large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats in South and South-east Asia. Known as Bhimanama in Kerala. Conservation status: WPA Schedule I, CITES Appendix II, IUCN Critically Endangered (CR). They are ambush predators with flattened heads.
evidenceBullets: Listed in WPA, 1972 Schedule I and CITES Appendix II
Found in large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats in South and South-east Asia
Ambush predators with aggressive behavior and a lightning speed strike

subtopic: Softshell Turtles and New Fish Species
title: Labeo uru and Labeo chekida
focus: Newly discovered freshwater fish species endemic to the Western Ghats river systems.
rationale: New species discoveries in biodiversity hotspots (Western Ghats) are standard UPSC prelims targets, especially matching species with their river habitats.
sectionExcerpt: ICAR-NBFGR discovered two new freshwater fish species endemic to Western Ghats river systems. Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River (originating from Pattimala hills, Karnataka). Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river (originating in Anamalai Hills).
evidenceBullets: Discovered by ICAR-National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources (NBFGR)
Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River, a west-flowing interstate river
Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river formed in the Anamalai Hills
Both are freshwater fish endemic to the Western Ghats river systems
